mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment defined by high unemployment, accelerating payroll growth signals a strengthening labor market and improving corporate earnings prospects, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Current unemployment rate of 9.4% indicates significant labor market slack consistent with a post-shock recovery phase.
failure_condition: Periods of accelerating PAYEMS growth while UNRATE is above 7% are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate near the zero lower bound, highly accommodative monetary policy provides a persistent tailwind for equities by lowering the discount rate for future cash flows and encouraging risk-taking. This effect is always active while rates remain exceptionally low.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% reflects a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) environment.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% is not statistically significantly higher than when it is above 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in industrial production reflects a strengthening supply side of the economy, indicating robust business activity and higher future earnings, which drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts below a critical threshold indicates a deep slump in a key sector of the economy, negatively impacting broader economic activity and investor sentiment, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST value of 555.0 is historically low, indicating a severe housing market downturn.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of the months where the HOUST level is below 600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the Consumer Price Index signals emerging inflation, which could force the central bank to signal future monetary tightening, negatively impacting equity valuations through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: Triggered periods are not followed by negative 60-day returns on average over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising inflation (cost-push pressures) and slowing industrial production signals a stagflationary environment. This dynamic is highly negative for corporate profit margins and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of a model using this combined signal is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) during periods that meet the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is high, the central bank can maintain an accommodative policy without fearing wage-price inflation. This interaction of labor market slack and easy money is bullish for equities, as it implies policy support will remain for an extended period.
evidence_refs: fact:Current state (UNRATE=9.4, FEDFUNDS=0.18) meets the trigger condition.
failure_condition: A strategy of buying the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met does not generate a statistically significant positive alpha over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which is a primary driver of corporate revenues. This negative signal leads to lower equity prices over a very short horizon as investors react to the new data.
failure_condition: Instances of negative monthly retail sales growth are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.
